commands.review_summary:
The branch changes `src/agents/live-model-switch.ts` to treat an active `openai-codex` session as already matching a canonical `openai` selection for the same model, with focused regression tests for switch and no-switch cases.

PR surface: Source +20, Tests +86. Total +106 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main compares raw provider strings and the runner throws `LiveSessionModelSwitchError` on a requested switch; the provided report includes matching gateway log evidence. I did not run the live Codex path in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: Human/contributor action is needed to supply after-fix real behavior proof; no narrow automated code repair is identified.

target.title: fix(agents): use runtime alias equivalence in live model switch comparison
target.body:
## Summary

- `hasDifferentLiveSessionModelSelection` used raw `!==` to compare provider IDs, so when the runtime promoted `openai` → `openai-codex` (the normal codex app-server startup path), the live-switch check saw a mismatch against the config-resolved `openai` provider and threw `LiveSessionModelSwitchError` — killing the active codex turn mid-execution with no user action.
- Replace the raw string comparison with `areRuntimeModelRefsEquivalent`, which already handles the `openai-codex ↔ openai` equivalence via `RUNTIME_COMPARISON_PROVIDER_ALIASES`.

## Context

This is a targeted fix for a deeper architectural issue: the codebase conflates provider identity with runtime identity by promoting `openai` → `openai-codex` at session start (in `resolveSelectedOpenAIPiRuntimeProvider`, `resolveContextConfigProviderForRuntime`, and related functions in `openai-codex-routing.ts`). The proper long-term fix is to stop mutating the provider ID and track runtime selection separately, then retire the `openai-codex` provider alias entirely. That refactor touches auth profile resolution, compaction routing, context config, and auth order lookup across `openai-codex-routing.ts`, `model-runtime-aliases.ts`, and all their callers.

## Reproduction

1. Configure `openai/gpt-5.5` as the default model (provider = `openai`)
2. Start a session that uses the codex app-server runtime (runtime promotes provider to `openai-codex`)
3. Wait for a retry loop boundary where `shouldSwitchToLiveModel` fires
4. The config-resolved selection returns `openai/gpt-5.5`, the running session reports `openai-codex/gpt-5.5`
5. `hasDifferentLiveSessionModelSelection` returns `true` → `LiveSessionModelSwitchError` thrown → active turn killed

Gateway log evidence:
```
16:00:42 [agent/embedded] live session model switch requested during active attempt
  for 633a9e17: openai-codex/gpt-5.5 -> openai/gpt-5.5
```

## Test plan

- [x] New test: `openai-codex` and `openai` treated as equivalent runtime providers (bidirectional)
- [x] New test: model differences still trigger switch across runtime alias
- [x] New test: `shouldSwitchToLiveModel` does not fire when runtime promotes `openai` → `openai-codex`
- [x] All 44 existing tests pass

Fixes #87226.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
